where the difrence oerators $\partial_{t}$ ,$\overline{{\partial}}_{t}$ are the discrtiation of $\frac{\partial}{\partial t}$ an e fe eraors $\partial_{\mathsf{x}_{1}},\overline{{\partial}}_{\mathsf{x}_{1}}$ are the discretization of $\frac{\partial}{\partial x_{1}}$ . Via these notations and the properties of the diference operators6 we present the statement of the local/global conservation laws for the different numerical methods
3.1. Symplectic method for Maxwell's equations

The following method i costructed based on the method of liesi.e.dicretizing the Hamitonian PDEs i space, then applying  m hd    e r xa).e l w e is also mutimplectic in the cresponding stamn of multiymplecticity.

\partial_{t}z_{i j,k}^{l}+M^{-1}K_{1}\overline{{\partial}}_{x_{1}}z_{i j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+M^{-1}K_{2}\overline{{\partial}}_{x_{2}}z_{i j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+M^{-1}K_{3}\overline{{\partial}}_{x_{3}}z_{i j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}=0,
where indices $i,j,$ $K_{1},\cdots$ .as in (10). We refer to this particular discretization as the symplectic method, though it is also multisymplectic.
The symplectic method (18) is second-order in space and time, and is unconditionally stable.Furthermore, this discrete st d on (8),
\frac{1}{2}\partial_{t}\left[\mu\mathbf{H}_{i j,k}^{l}\cdot\mathbf{H}_{i j,k}^{l}+\varepsilon\mathbf{E}_{i j,k}^{l}\cdot\mathbf{E}_{i j,k}^{l}\right]=0.
The second discretized global conservation law for symplectic method is based on the helicity Hamiltonian functional (6)

\partial_{t}\left[\frac{1}{2\varepsilon}\mathbf{H}_{i j,k}^{l}\cdot\widehat{\nabla}\times\mathbf{H}_{i j,k}^{l}+\frac{1}{2\mu}\mathbf{E}_{i j,k}^{l}\cdot\widehat{\nabla}\times\mathbf{E}_{i j,k}^{l}\right]=0,

where $\widehat{\nabla}\times=R_{1}\partial_{x_{1}}+R_{2}\partial_{x_{2}}+R_{3}\partial_{x_{3}}$ .Furthermore, the scheme (1 i proved to be multiymplectic, since it preseres the following multisymplectic conservation law

\begin{align*}\partial_{t}\Big[&d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l}\wedge d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l}\Big]+\partial_{x_1}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij-1,j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_1d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{i-1,j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_1d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]+\partial_{x_2}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_2d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{ij-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_2d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]\\&+\partial_{x_3}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_3d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_3d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]=0.\end{align*} 21)

The discrete quadratic conservation law is

\begin{align*}&\frac{1}{2}\partial_t\Big[\mu\mathbf{H}^{l}_{ij,k}\cdot\mathbf{H}^{l}_{ij,k}+\varepsilon\mathbf{E}^{l}_{ij,k}\cdot\mathbf{E}^{l}_{ij,k}\Big]+\frac{1}{2}\partial_{x_1}\Big[\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\cdot R_1\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{i-1j,k}+\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{i-1j,k}\cdot R_1\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\Big]+\frac{1}{2}\partial_{x_2}\Big[\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\cdot R_2\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij-1,k}+\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij-1,k}\cdot R_2\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\Big]\\&\quad+\frac{1}{2}\partial_{x_3}\Big[\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\cdot R_3\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k-1}+\mathbf{H}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k-1}\cdot R_3\mathbf{E}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}_{ij,k}\Big]=\mathbf{0}.\end{align*}
The discrete energy conservation law is

\begin{array}{r l}&{\partial_{t}\bigg[\cfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}\mathbf{H}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot\widehat{\nabla}\times\mathbf{H}_{i j k}^{l}+\cfrac{1}{2\mu}\mathbf{E}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot\widehat{\nabla}\times\mathbf{E}_{i j k}^{l}\bigg]+\partial_{x_{1}}\bigg[\cfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}\partial_{t}\mathbf{H}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{1}\mathbf{H}_{i-1,j k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\cfrac{1}{2\mu}\partial_{t}\mathbf{E}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{1}\mathbf{E}_{i-1,j k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\bigg]}\\ &{\quad+\partial_{x_{2}}\bigg[\cfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}\partial_{t}\mathbf{H}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{2}\mathbf{H}_{i j-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\cfrac{1}{2\mu}\partial_{t}\mathbf{E}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{2}\mathbf{E}_{i j-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\bigg]+\partial_{x_{3}}\bigg[\cfrac{1}{2\varepsilon}\partial_{t}\mathbf{H}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{3}\mathbf{H}_{i j k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\cfrac{1}{2\mu}\partial_{t}\mathbf{E}_{i j k}^{l}\cdot R_{3}\mathbf{E}_{i j,k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\bigg]=\mathbf{0}.}\end{array}
The discrete momentum conservation law is

$\begin{align*}\partial_{t}\Big[&d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l}\wedge d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l}\Big]+\partial_{x_1}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij-1,j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_1d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{i-1,j,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_1d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]+\partial_{x_2}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_2d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{ij-1,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_2d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]\\&+\partial_{x_3}\left[\frac{1}{\bar{c}}d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_3d\mathbb{H}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}+\frac{1}{\mu}d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k-1}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\wedge R_3d\mathbb{E}_{ij,k}^{l+\frac{1}{2}}\right]=0.\end{align*}$ 21)
